title: Count Distinct Subsequences
description:
Given strings s and t, return the number of distinct subsequences of s which equals t. Empty string t is always one subsequence of any s.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ |s| ≤ 1000
- 1 ≤ |t| ≤ 1000
explanation:
Intuition
dp[i][j] = number of ways s[:i] forms t[:j]. If s[i-1]==t[j-1], we can match them or skip s[i-1]. Otherwise only skip s[i-1].

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize dp[0][0..m] with dp[0][>0]=0 and dp[*][0]=1
2. Main Algorithm: Fill table with character match rule
3. Return Result: Return dp[n][m]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(nm)
- Space Complexity: O(nm) or O(m) rolling

Key Insights
- When chars match: add both take and skip cases
- Empty t has exactly 1 subsequence

Common Pitfalls
- Using permutations instead of subsequences is incorrect
- Beware integer growth; Python handles big ints

testCases.explanation: Three ways to drop extra b
